title: Ordering Trees by Their ABC Spectral Radii
author_string: Wenshui Lin, Zhangyong Yan, Peifang Fu, Jia-Bao Liu
abstract: Let $G=(V,E)$ be a connected graph, where $V=\{v_1, v_2, \cdots, v_n\}$. Let $d_i$ denote the degree of vertex $v_i$. The ABC matrix of $G$ is defined as $M(G)=(m_{ij})_{n \times n}$, where $m_{ij}=\sqrt{(d_i + d_j -2)/(d_i d_j)}$ if $v_i v_j \in E$, and 0 otherwise. The ABC spectral radius of $G$ is the largest eigenvalue of $M(G)$. In the present paper, we establish two graph perturbations with respect to ABC spectral radius. By applying these perturbations, the trees with the third, fourth, and fifth largest ABC spectral radii are determined.
